title: Choose location and date
description: Scout a site with visible litter, safe access and parking; pick a weekend date 3-4 weeks out.

title: Get landowner or municipal permission
description: Contact the municipality or landowner to confirm the cleanup is allowed and ask about waste collection support.

title: Arrange waste disposal
description: Agree with the local waste service where filled bags will be collected and how recyclables are separated.

title: Gather equipment
description: Source gloves, litter pickers, bags and hi-vis vests; many municipalities lend these for free.

title: Promote the event
description: Create a simple flyer and post in local social media groups, noticeboards and schools.

title: Prepare a safety briefing
description: Write a short briefing covering sharps, traffic, weather and hygiene; identify a first-aider.

title: Run the cleanup
description: Register attendees, brief them, assign zones, and coordinate collection points during the event.

title: Document and share results
description: Weigh or count bags, photograph the haul and thank volunteers publicly to build momentum for the next one.

title: Define scope and format
description: Decide which repair categories to offer first (e.g. electronics, textiles, bikes) and target visitor numbers.

title: Recruit volunteer fixers
description: Find 4-8 skilled repairers via community groups, makerspaces and retirement networks; confirm their categories.

title: Secure a venue
description: Book a community hall or café with tables, power sockets and good lighting for a half-day slot.

title: Sort insurance and liability
description: Check the venue's public liability cover and prepare a simple repair disclaimer form for visitors.

title: Assemble tools and spares
description: Collect basic toolkits, multimeters, sewing machines and common spare parts; ask fixers what they need.

title: Promote locally
description: Design a poster, list the event on local calendars and invite people to register items in advance.

title: Run the event
description: Manage the welcome desk, queue visitors to the right fixer, and log each item and outcome.

title: Log repairs and follow up
description: Record repair statistics (items fixed, waste diverted), thank fixers and announce the next date.

title: Set format and rules
description: Decide the swap mechanics (token per item vs. free-for-all), quality standards and item limits.

title: Book a venue
description: Find a free or cheap space with tables, mirrors and a changing corner.

title: Promote and manage sign-ups
description: Publicise via social media and posters; use a simple sign-up form to estimate attendance.

title: Organise intake and sorting
description: Plan how items are checked in, quality-screened and displayed by size and type.

title: Run the swap
description: Staff the intake desk, keep rails tidy, and handle the token system during the event.

title: Handle leftovers responsibly
description: Arrange donation of unclaimed items to a charity shop or textile recycler.

title: Pick season and venue
description: Schedule for early spring or autumn and book a hall, market corner or community garden.

title: Set swap guidelines
description: Write simple rules on labelling, invasive species to exclude, and seed packaging.

title: Recruit experienced gardeners
description: Invite 2-3 knowledgeable gardeners to answer questions and identify plants on the day.

title: Promote to gardening networks
description: Share in allotment associations, balcony-gardening groups and local media.

title: Run the swap
description: Set up labelled tables by plant type, manage intake and keep the exchange flowing.

title: Capture the community
description: Collect contacts of attendees who want a recurring swap or a future community garden.

title: Select shoreline site and tide/weather window
description: Choose an accessible stretch of shore and a date with a fallback option for bad weather.

title: Coordinate with authorities
description: Inform the municipality or lake authority, confirm access rules and waste pickup.

title: Plan litter data collection
description: Adopt a simple litter survey protocol so the cleanup contributes data to marine litter research.

title: Gather equipment and safety gear
description: Source gloves, bags, buckets for sorting, and sun/water safety equipment.

title: Promote the event
description: Publicise with strong before-photos of the site; partner with local water-sports clubs.

title: Run cleanup and sort waste
description: Brief volunteers, assign zones, and sort collected waste into recyclables and residual.

title: Report results
description: Submit litter data, publish totals and photos, and thank partners and volunteers.

title: Map fruit trees and recruit tree owners
description: Invite owners to register trees they cannot harvest; log location, species and ripening window.

title: Set the sharing agreement
description: Define the split (e.g. thirds to owner, pickers, charity) and a simple liability waiver for climbing/ladders.

title: Gather harvesting equipment
description: Source ladders, picking bags, crates and fruit pickers; arrange storage between harvests.

title: Recruit and schedule pickers
description: Build a picker list and match small teams to trees as they ripen.

title: Run harvest sessions
description: Lead safe picking sessions, weigh the harvest and split according to the agreement.

title: Distribute surplus
description: Deliver the charity share to a food bank or community fridge and log totals.

title: Secure surplus food sources
description: Agree with supermarkets, bakeries or markets to donate unsellable but safe produce.

title: Book venue with kitchen access
description: Find a public square or hall with cooking facilities, water and power.

title: Plan the menu and hygiene protocol
description: Design flexible recipes for unpredictable ingredients and set food-safety rules for volunteers.

title: Recruit cooking volunteers and a lead cook
description: Find an experienced cook to lead plus 6-10 helpers for chopping, cooking and serving.

title: Promote as a festive public event
description: Market it as a party with purpose: music, free food, and a message about food waste.

title: Run the event
description: Coordinate food collection, communal preparation, serving and the atmosphere on the day.

title: Share impact
description: Publish kilograms of food rescued and portions served; credit food donors visibly.

title: Choose venue and format
description: Book a library, school hall or café and decide swap rules and item quality standards.

title: Promote to families
description: Reach parents via schools, kindergartens and neighbourhood groups.

title: Organise intake and display
description: Plan quality checks and arrange items by age group and category.

title: Run the swap
description: Manage intake, keep displays tidy and host a small reading or play corner.

title: Donate leftovers
description: Pass unclaimed items to a charity, hospital or refugee support organisation.

title: Partner with a certified recycler
description: Agree with a licensed e-waste recycler or municipal service to take the collected items.

title: Choose a visible drop-off site
description: Book a car park, school yard or market corner with easy drive-up access for a Saturday morning.

title: Plan data-security messaging
description: Prepare guidance on wiping personal data from devices before donation.

title: Promote with a clear accepted-items list
description: Publish exactly what is and is not accepted to avoid being dumped with hazardous items.

title: Run the collection
description: Staff intake, sort items into recycler categories and handle batteries safely.

title: Report the haul
description: Publish total weight collected and, where possible, devices routed to reuse rather than recycling.

title: Recruit a naturalist guide
description: Find a local ecologist, birder or botanist willing to lead identification.

title: Choose site and get access permission
description: Pick a park, reserve or riverbank and confirm group access with the manager.

title: Set up the recording tool
description: Create an event project in a citizen-science app (e.g. iNaturalist) and prepare a how-to guide.

title: Promote to families and schools
description: Frame it as a nature treasure hunt; invite school classes and family groups.

title: Run the BioBlitz
description: Lead the walk, help participants photograph and log species, and keep groups safe.

title: Publish the species count
description: Share the final species tally and highlight notable finds with the community and the site manager.

title: Design build kits
description: Choose species-appropriate designs and prepare cutting lists sized for beginners.

title: Source materials and tools
description: Get untreated timber (ideally offcuts/donations), screws, drills and saws for group use.

title: Book a workshop space
description: Find a makerspace, school workshop or garage with workbenches and power.

title: Promote and take registrations
description: Cap participant numbers to match tools; promote to families and garden owners.

title: Run the build session
description: Teach safe tool use, guide assembly and explain correct placement for each design.

title: Coordinate installation
description: Help participants site boxes correctly and arrange installing a few in public green spaces.

title: Choose location and get permission
description: Find a sheltered, visible, accessible spot and get written approval from the municipality or landowner.

title: Design a weatherproof cabinet
description: Design or adapt a robust, rainproof design with clear signage space.

title: Source materials or an old cabinet
description: Get donated materials or upcycle an old wardrobe/phone box; budget for weatherproofing.

title: Build the box
description: Construct and weatherproof the cabinet over one or two build days.

title: Install and launch
description: Mount it securely, stock it with starter items and hold a small opening moment for the neighbourhood.

title: Set up light-touch stewardship
description: Recruit 2-3 nearby residents to tidy it weekly and post house rules inside.

title: Identify a site and get approval
description: Find an underused green strip and get permission from the municipality or landowner, including a no-mow agreement.

title: Choose native seed mix
description: Select a regionally native, pollinator-friendly seed mix suited to the soil and light conditions.

title: Plan site preparation
description: Determine how the ground will be cleared and prepared, and what tools are needed.

title: Recruit volunteers and promote
description: Invite neighbours and families; frame it as creating a butterfly meadow on their street.

title: Run the planting day
description: Prepare soil, sow seeds or plant plugs, water in, and install a small explanatory sign.

title: Arrange aftercare
description: Set a light watering/weeding rota for the establishment period and agree the mowing regime.

title: Choose film and secure screening rights
description: Pick an engaging film and obtain a public screening licence or use a free-to-screen title.

title: Book venue with projection
description: Find a café, library or hall with projector, sound and seating.

title: Design the discussion format
description: Prepare 3-4 discussion questions that connect the film to concrete local action options.

title: Promote the evening
description: Publicise via local calendars, posters and partner organisations.

title: Host the screening and discussion
description: Welcome guests, run the screening, and facilitate the conversation toward next steps.

title: Capture interest for follow-up projects
description: Collect contacts of attendees who want to act, and connect them to concrete projects.

title: Recruit 1-2 sewing mentors
description: Find experienced menders willing to teach basics like darning, patching and button repair.

title: Find a regular cosy venue
description: Book a recurring slot in a café, library or community room with good light and tables.

title: Assemble a shared mending kit
description: Collect needles, threads, yarns, patches and a couple of sewing machines.

title: Promote the first sessions
description: Emphasise the social, creative angle; ask people to bring one damaged garment.

title: Host the first circle
description: Run intros, demonstrate a basic technique, and support everyone to finish one repair.

title: Establish the recurring rhythm
description: Fix a monthly schedule, rotate hosting duties and build a message group for regulars.

title: Gauge interest and form a core group
description: Confirm 5-10 committed neighbours and agree the garden's purpose (food, community, education).

title: Find and assess land
description: Identify candidate plots; check sunlight, water access, soil contamination history and ownership.

title: Negotiate land use agreement
description: Agree a written multi-year use agreement with the municipality or landowner.

title: Design the garden layout
description: Plan beds, paths, compost area, water points and a communal space; decide plot vs. communal model.

title: Budget and fundraise for setup
description: Cost soil, timber, tools and water connection; raise funds via small grants or crowdfunding.

title: Organise build days
description: Run 2-3 volunteer days to build beds, paths and compost bins.

title: Set governance and rules
description: Agree membership, fees, plot allocation, shared duties and conflict resolution.

title: Plant the first season
description: Coordinate a planting plan and first sowing with all members.

title: Establish ongoing rhythm
description: Set up watering rotas, monthly workdays and a communication channel.

title: Assess demand and site options
description: Survey neighbours on interest and find a site with drainage and reasonable distance from homes.

title: Get municipal approval
description: Clarify local rules on composting sites, odour and vermin control, and get written permission.

title: Design the composting system
description: Choose the system (3-bay, tumblers, bokashi) sized for expected volume, and plan the material flow.

title: Build the composting site
description: Construct bins, signage and a covered browns storage area over 1-2 build days.

title: Train compost stewards
description: Train 3-5 volunteers in layering, turning, temperature checks and troubleshooting.

title: Onboard households
description: Register participating households, distribute caddies and a clear what-goes-in guide.

title: Run and monitor the first cycles
description: Operate a steward rota, monitor quality and fix issues quickly to keep neighbours onside.

title: Distribute finished compost
description: Hold a compost giveaway day for members and the local community garden.

title: Validate demand and model
description: Survey the neighbourhood on desired items and willingness to pay a small membership fee.

title: Find a home for the collection
description: Secure a small storage space with public access hours — a shop corner, cellar or container.

title: Sort legal structure and insurance
description: Set up a simple association, liability terms for borrowers and insurance for the inventory.

title: Collect and catalogue inventory
description: Run a donation drive for quality tools; test, tag and catalogue each item.

title: Set up a booking system
description: Deploy lightweight lending software (several open-source options exist) with member accounts and due dates.

title: Recruit and train volunteer librarians
description: Build a rota of volunteers to staff opening hours and check items in/out.

title: Launch with an open day
description: Hold a launch event with demos, sign-ups and a tool amnesty for more donations.

title: Establish maintenance routines
description: Set up repair days for damaged items and periodic inventory checks.

title: Find a host location
description: Secure a sheltered, accessible spot with power — outside a shop, community centre or church.

title: Clarify food-safety rules
description: Define what may be shared (no raw meat/fish, date rules) following established community-fridge guidelines.

title: Source a fridge and shelter
description: Get a donated or cheap reliable fridge and build a simple weatherproof housing if outdoors.

title: Recruit fridge stewards
description: Build a rota of volunteers for daily checks, cleaning and temperature logging.

title: Onboard food donors
description: Sign up nearby bakeries, shops and households as regular contributors.

title: Launch and publicise
description: Open with clear signage on rules, and publicise so both givers and takers feel welcome.

title: Monitor and adapt
description: Track usage and hygiene, fix problems fast and share impact numbers monthly.

title: Map donors and recipients
description: List potential food donors (bakeries, supermarkets, caterers) and recipients (shelters, fridges, food banks).

title: Agree donation terms
description: Sign simple agreements covering pickup times, food types and liability with each donor.

title: Design safe transport routine
description: Plan routes, cool boxes and hygiene rules so food stays safe in transit.

title: Recruit rescue riders/drivers
description: Build a pool of volunteers with bikes or cars who each take one short weekly slot.

title: Set up shift scheduling
description: Use a simple scheduling tool so every pickup slot is reliably covered or escalated.

title: Run a pilot month
description: Operate 2-3 routes for a month, log weights and iron out timing problems.

title: Scale and report
description: Add donors and routes, and publish monthly kilograms rescued to keep partners engaged.

title: Design the curriculum
description: Define 4-6 sessions with clear skills outcomes per session and required materials.

title: Recruit instructors
description: Find skilled repairers able to teach each module; agree dates and any stipends.

title: Book workshop venue
description: Secure a space with workbenches, power and tool storage for the series.

title: Source practice materials
description: Collect broken donor devices/garments for participants to practise on.

title: Recruit participants
description: Open registration with a small commitment fee to reduce no-shows.

title: Deliver the sessions
description: Run each session with demos, guided practice and safety supervision.

title: Graduate fixers into repair cafés
description: Connect graduates to local repair cafés as volunteer fixers.

title: Win school buy-in
description: Pitch the programme to the head and 1-2 champion teachers; agree goals and responsibilities.

title: Design an age-appropriate garden
description: Plan raised beds, safe tools and crops that fruit within school terms.

title: Fund the build
description: Raise a small budget through the parents' association, local sponsors or a mini-grant.

title: Build with the school community
description: Run a family build day to construct beds and fill soil.

title: Create simple lesson links
description: Prepare seasonal activity sheets linking garden tasks to the curriculum.

title: Support the first growing year
description: Mentor teachers through sowing, care and harvest across the school year.

title: Plan holiday care and handover
description: Set up a holiday watering rota and progressively hand ownership to the school.

title: Identify planting sites
description: Survey the neighbourhood for viable spots considering utilities, sightlines and space for mature trees.

title: Get municipal approval and species advice
description: Work with the city's green department on permissions and climate-adapted native species.

title: Fund the trees
description: Cost saplings, stakes and soil; raise funds via sponsorship (e.g. adopt-a-tree) or grants.

title: Recruit resident tree stewards
description: Sign up nearby residents to water and watch over specific trees for the first two summers.

title: Organise the planting day
description: Coordinate delivery, tools, pit preparation and a correct-planting demo.

title: Plant and celebrate
description: Plant with residents, mulch and stake properly, and mark the day with the neighbourhood.

title: Run the aftercare programme
description: Coordinate watering reminders and check-ups through the critical establishment period.

title: Form the founding group
description: Gather 8-15 households and agree product priorities and order frequency.

title: Select suppliers
description: Research wholesalers and producers offering bulk, low-packaging staples at good prices.

title: Set up ordering and payments
description: Create a shared order sheet and a transparent payment/settlement process.

title: Organise distribution point
description: Arrange a garage or community room for delivery, splitting and pickup.

title: Run the first order cycle
description: Collect orders, place the bulk order, split goods into member portions and settle payments.

title: Refine and formalise
description: Review pricing and effort after 2-3 cycles; agree roles and a small margin for shared costs.

title: Define the community and routes
description: Pick a bounded community with repeated journeys (commutes, training sessions, school runs).

title: Choose a matching mechanism
description: Select a simple tool — an existing carpool app, a group chat with pinned routes, or a shared sheet.

title: Set ground rules
description: Agree cost-sharing norms, reliability expectations and insurance basics for drivers.

title: Recruit a critical mass
description: Sign up enough drivers and riders on the core routes for matches to actually occur.

title: Launch and seed the first matches
description: Manually broker the first pairings so early users experience success.

title: Monitor and celebrate savings
description: Track shared rides and publicise kilometres and CO2 saved to sustain participation.

title: Partner with the school
description: Get school endorsement and help contacting interested families.

title: Design safe routes and stops
description: Map 1-3 walking routes with timed pickup stops, avoiding dangerous crossings.

title: Recruit parent walkers
description: Sign up enough adult volunteers for a reliable rota with backups.

title: Set safety protocol
description: Agree adult-child ratios, registration procedure, absence handling and bad-weather rules.

title: Pilot for two weeks
description: Run the routes daily, adjust timings and fix pain points.

title: Formalise the rota
description: Move to a stable schedule with a coordinator per route and a substitution system.

title: Recruit sewing instructors
description: Find 2-3 skilled sewists/designers to lead project-based sessions.

title: Secure machines and space
description: Book a venue and source 6-10 working sewing machines (borrowed or donated).

title: Collect donor textiles
description: Run a textile donation drive and sort usable fabric by type.

title: Design beginner-friendly projects
description: Prepare 3-4 projects (tote from jeans, patchwork cushion) with patterns and steps.

title: Promote and register participants
description: Market the series and manage sign-ups sized to machine availability.

title: Deliver the workshops
description: Teach the sessions with per-machine support and safety guidance.

title: Showcase results
description: Hold a small exhibition or online gallery of finished pieces to recruit the next cohort.

title: Find a kitchen venue
description: Book a community kitchen or rotate between members' homes for monthly sessions.

title: Set the format
description: Agree the model: shared cost, rotating menu themes, and one host-cook per session.

title: Plan seasonal menus
description: Draft simple seasonal menus with shopping lists sized per group.

title: Recruit members
description: Invite a mixed group and emphasise the food-first, non-preachy framing.

title: Host the first sessions
description: Cook and eat together, sharing techniques and recipe cards.

title: Publish the recipe collection
description: Compile tested recipes into a shared booklet or webpage for wider reach.

title: Define categories and coverage area
description: Agree the directory scope: repair, reuse, refill, sharing, food rescue.

title: Crowdsource and research entries
description: Split the area among volunteers to research and verify entries by phone or visit.

title: Build the map
description: Publish entries on an open map platform with categories and descriptions.

title: Verify with listed businesses
description: Contact each business to confirm details and permission to be listed.

title: Design a printable version
description: Create a printable one-page map/flyer for libraries and cafés.

title: Launch and maintain
description: Promote the map and set a twice-yearly verification routine.

title: Build resident support
description: Door-knock the street to gather signatures supporting a temporary closure.

title: Apply for the street closure permit
description: Navigate the municipal permit process including traffic plan and notification requirements.

title: Plan activities and safety
description: Organise games, chalk zones, a shared table, and barriers/marshals for the closure points.

title: Borrow equipment
description: Source barriers, signage, games and furniture from the municipality and neighbours.

title: Promote to the whole street
description: Letterbox every household with the date and how to get involved.

title: Run the day
description: Manage setup, marshals, activities and teardown.

title: Gather momentum
description: Survey attendees on making it regular and share results with the municipality.

title: Define the measure kit
description: Choose safe, high-impact measures: draft strips, radiator foil, LED swaps, thermostat guidance.

title: Train the brigade
description: Run a hands-on training day covering measures, measurement and respectful home visits.

title: Fund the materials
description: Buy kits in bulk via a small grant, sponsorship or cost-price contributions.

title: Recruit households
description: Prioritise energy-poor households via community organisations and simple sign-up.

title: Set visit protocol
description: Agree two-person visits, consent forms, and what the brigade will and won't touch (no gas/mains).

title: Run visit rounds
description: Schedule and complete home visits, logging measures installed per home.

title: Measure and report savings
description: Estimate energy/cost savings per home and report results to funders and community.

title: Identify recyclable streams and takers
description: Research which hard-to-recycle streams have genuine local recycling routes and confirm the receivers.

title: Recruit host locations
description: Sign up cafés, shops and offices willing to host labelled collection boxes.

title: Design and place boxes
description: Create clearly labelled, tidy collection boxes with accepted-items graphics.

title: Organise the emptying rota
description: Set volunteer routes to empty boxes and deliver materials to recyclers monthly.

title: Publicise the network
description: Publish a map of points and what each accepts.

title: Track volumes and expand
description: Log collected volumes, drop dead streams, add popular ones and new locations.

title: Map official drop-off points
description: Compile every local battery/bulb return point into a simple map.

title: Create campaign materials
description: Design posters and a one-pager explaining why and where, in plain language.

title: Distribute through local channels
description: Place materials in shops, schools and building lobbies; post in neighbourhood groups.

title: Run a collection booster week
description: Pair the campaign with a one-week household amnesty drive at a visible point.

title: Report results
description: Share collected volumes and keep the map alive as a lasting resource.

title: Scout neglected spots
description: Identify tree pits, verges and abandoned planters where planting would be welcomed.

title: Check the legal position
description: Understand local rules; where possible seek informal blessing from the municipality to avoid removal.

title: Choose hardy native plants
description: Select drought-tolerant, non-invasive natives that survive neglect.

title: Make seed bombs together
description: Host a fun seed-bomb-making session as a recruitment activity.

title: Plant and adopt spots
description: Plant the sites and have each participant adopt one spot for occasional watering.

title: Secure venue and permit
description: Book a square, hall or street section and obtain the market permit.

title: Recruit sellers
description: Open stall registration with a small fee covering costs.

title: Plan site layout and logistics
description: Map stall pitches, access, waste stations and wet-weather plan.

title: Promote to buyers
description: Publicise through posters, socials and local press for footfall.

title: Run the market day
description: Manage setup, stall allocation, visitors and teardown.

title: Handle unsold goods
description: Offer a donation point so unsold items go to charity rather than home or bin.

title: Partner with a venue
description: Agree a quiet-night slot with a pub or café that benefits from the crowd.

title: Write the quiz
description: Create fun, surprising rounds that inform without lecturing.

title: Arrange small prizes
description: Get sustainable prizes donated by local businesses.

title: Promote the night
description: Publicise to reach beyond the usual green audience.

title: Host the quiz
description: MC the evening, score rounds and plug local projects between rounds.

title: Define editorial focus
description: Set the beat: local projects, events calendar, practical guides, interviews.

title: Set up the publishing platform
description: Choose a newsletter/blog platform and design a simple template.

title: Build a contributor pool
description: Recruit 3-5 writers/photographers who each cover one piece per issue.

title: Publish the first issues
description: Write, edit and publish the first 2-3 issues on a fixed schedule.

title: Grow the audience
description: Cross-promote with local groups and gather reader feedback.

title: Choose physical vs digital format
description: Decide between a weatherproof physical board, a chat group, or both.

title: Get placement permission
description: For a physical board, agree a spot with a shop, building manager or municipality.

title: Build/set up the board
description: Construct and mount the board or configure the digital group with clear categories and rules.

title: Seed initial content
description: Ask 10-15 neighbours to post first offers so it never looks empty.

title: Moderate and maintain
description: Keep content fresh and appropriate with a light monthly tidy-up.

title: Form the founding team
description: Assemble a core team covering technical, legal, financial and communications skills.

title: Choose the model
description: Decide between a group-purchase scheme (bulk discount for homeowners) and a cooperative owning shared installations.

title: Understand the regulatory framework
description: Research local rules on grid connection, feed-in remuneration and self-consumption communities.

title: Survey interest and roof potential
description: Register interested households/roof owners and pre-assess roof suitability.

title: Set up the legal entity
description: Found the cooperative or association with statutes, board and bank account.

title: Tender to installers
description: Run a competitive tender for a qualified installer with member pricing.

title: Arrange financing
description: Structure member shares, bank finance or subsidy applications to fund installations.

title: Run the member campaign
description: Hold info evenings and enrol members with clear contracts and expected returns.

title: Oversee installations
description: Coordinate site assessments, contracts and installation quality across member roofs.

title: Set up operations and reporting
description: Establish monitoring, maintenance contracts and annual member reporting.

title: Build the municipal relationship
description: Win support from the municipality for hosting citizen-financed solar on a public roof.

title: Shortlist and assess roofs
description: Technically assess candidate roofs for structure, orientation and grid connection.

title: Develop the business case
description: Model costs, generation, tariffs and citizen-investor returns over 20-25 years.

title: Establish the legal vehicle
description: Create the cooperative/company and the roof-lease and power-purchase agreements with the municipality.

title: Raise citizen investment
description: Run a community share offer with clear prospectus and subscription process.

title: Procure and build
description: Tender, contract and supervise the installation through to commissioning.

title: Launch publicly
description: Celebrate the switch-on publicly to build appetite for the next roof.

title: Operate and report
description: Run monitoring, maintenance, insurance and annual investor reporting.

title: Develop the audit methodology
description: Create a standardised walk-through checklist and reporting template with an energy professional.

title: Address liability
description: Set clear advisory-only terms, disclaimers and insurance for volunteers entering homes.

title: Train volunteer auditors
description: Deliver training with supervised practice audits before solo visits.

title: Build the referral pipeline
description: Partner with the municipality and social organisations to reach households, prioritising energy poverty.

title: Schedule and run audits
description: Book two-person visits, complete audits and deliver written recommendations.

title: Connect to follow-up support
description: Link households to subsidy programmes, installers and the draft-proofing brigade.

title: Evaluate impact
description: Follow up on implemented measures and estimate aggregate savings.

title: Identify a suitable paved site
description: Find over-paved space (school yard corner, parking edge) where infiltration would help.

title: Get technical and permit approval
description: Confirm no utilities below, drainage suitability and municipal permission to depave.

title: Design the rain garden
description: Design basin depth, overflow, soil profile and water-tolerant native planting.

title: Fund materials and machinery
description: Budget for machinery hire, soil, gravel and plants; seek a green-infrastructure grant.

title: Organise the depave day
description: Break and remove paving with proper tools, safety gear and disposal of rubble.

title: Build and plant the garden
description: Shape the basin, amend soil and plant over one or two community days.

title: Monitor performance
description: Check infiltration after storms and maintain planting through year one.

title: Secure the host building
description: Agree with a community building owner to host a demonstration installation.

title: Commission structural assessment
description: Have an engineer verify load capacity and waterproofing condition.

title: Design the system
description: Choose extensive green roof or facade system, plant palette and irrigation approach.

title: Fund the pilot
description: Combine municipal green-building subsidies, sponsorship and crowdfunding.

title: Obtain permits
description: Secure building permission and any heritage/facade approvals.

title: Install with professionals and volunteers
description: Contract specialist work; involve volunteers in planting and finishing.

title: Use it as a demonstrator
description: Install monitoring and signage; host open visits to inspire replication.

title: Choose sensor platform
description: Select an established open-hardware sensor design with a public data platform.

title: Fund the sensor fleet
description: Budget 15-30 sensors and raise funds via micro-grants or host contributions.

title: Recruit sensor hosts
description: Sign up households/businesses across a representative grid with power and WiFi at mounting points.

title: Run sensor build workshops
description: Assemble and flash sensors together in soldering workshops.

title: Install and calibrate
description: Mount sensors correctly, connect to the data platform and cross-check readings.

title: Analyse and publish findings
description: Produce accessible analyses of hotspots and trends for residents and the municipality.

title: Maintain the network
description: Set a maintenance rota for offline or drifting sensors.

title: Assess the stream section
description: Document current condition, pollution sources and restoration potential with expert input.

title: Engage the water authority
description: Build a working relationship with the responsible authority; align on scope and permissions.

title: Develop the restoration plan
description: Plan invasive removal, native replanting, bank stabilisation and habitat features within permitted scope.

title: Fund the works
description: Apply for environmental grants and secure in-kind support from the authority.

title: Run volunteer work days
description: Deliver the manual works in supervised volunteer days across seasons.

title: Monitor recovery
description: Track indicator species and water quality before/after with citizen-science methods.

title: Report and celebrate
description: Publish results with the authority and mark completion with the community.

title: Map the corridor route
description: Identify a habitat connection route across private and public land.

title: Negotiate with landowners
description: Secure planting agreements with each landowner along the route.

title: Design species mix
description: Choose native hedgerow species for food, shelter and connectivity value.

title: Source plants and funding
description: Order bare-root whips in season and cover costs via biodiversity grants.

title: Run planting days
description: Plant sections with volunteers over the dormant season, with protection against browsing.

title: Establish maintenance agreements
description: Agree who maintains each section for the establishment years.

title: Monitor wildlife use
description: Set up simple wildlife monitoring (tracks, cameras, surveys) along the corridor.

title: Secure long-term land access
description: Negotiate a 10+ year agreement, since trees need years to bear fruit.

title: Design the orchard
description: Plan spacing, pollination partners and heritage local varieties with expert advice.

title: Fund trees and infrastructure
description: Budget trees, stakes, guards and water access; run adopt-a-tree sponsorships.

title: Prepare the site
description: Clear, mark out and prepare planting pits before the dormant season.

title: Hold the planting festival
description: Plant the orchard in a community celebration with correct technique demos.

title: Set up the care group
description: Train a stewardship group in formative pruning, watering and pest care.

title: Plan annual traditions
description: Establish blossom day and harvest festival to keep the community attached year-round.

title: Build the audit framework
description: Adapt a lightweight SME framework covering energy, waste, procurement and transport.

title: Recruit professional volunteers
description: Enlist volunteers with sustainability, engineering or finance backgrounds.

title: Pilot with friendly businesses
description: Test the audit with 2-3 supportive businesses and refine for time-poor owners.

title: Formalise the offer
description: Define scope, confidentiality terms and a professional report template.

title: Recruit client businesses
description: Promote via the business association and chamber of commerce.

title: Deliver audit cycles
description: Match volunteer pairs to businesses and deliver audits with prioritised action plans.

title: Showcase wins
description: Publish anonymised case studies of savings achieved to attract the next cohort.

title: Choose the operating model
description: Decide between joining an existing sharing platform/network and building a standalone scheme.

title: Fund the fleet
description: Finance 2-5 cargo bikes via grants, sponsorship from local businesses or municipal mobility funds.

title: Set up booking and access
description: Deploy a booking system and key handover solution (host locations or smart locks).

title: Recruit host stations
description: Sign up cafés/shops as bike homes handling handovers during opening hours.

title: Sort insurance and user terms
description: Arrange bike insurance and clear user agreements covering damage and liability.

title: Launch and onboard riders
description: Run try-out days teaching cargo bike handling and sign up members.

title: Maintain the fleet
description: Establish regular maintenance with a local bike shop or volunteer mechanics.

title: Track usage and expand
description: Monitor bookings and user feedback; add bikes/stations where demand is proven.

title: Found the group
description: Gather a committed core, agree scope (advocacy vs. assembly push) and working principles.

title: Research the policy landscape
description: Analyse the municipality's climate plans, budgets and decision calendar to find leverage points.

title: Define concrete asks
description: Formulate 2-3 specific, costed, achievable policy proposals rather than general demands.

title: Build a broad coalition
description: Ally with local organisations, businesses and scientists to widen legitimacy.

title: Engage decision-makers
description: Meet councillors and officials; present proposals constructively and persistently.

title: Mobilise public support
description: Run petitions, public meetings and local media work around the asks.

title: Track and sustain
description: Monitor decisions, celebrate wins publicly and maintain momentum between milestones.
